MASTER MOTION CALENDAR ON AUG-31-2016 IN 206, HEARING ON APPLICANT/APPELLANT MIEKO SAITO'S WAIVER OF COURT FEES AND COSTS. APPEARANCE BY APPLICANT. PHYLLIS OGATA, NON-REGISTERED JAPANESE INTERPRETER, WITH OATH ON FILE, PRESENT AND ASSISTED APPELLANT. COURT FINDS THAT A CERTIFIED INTERPRETER IS NOT AVAILABLE AND APPOINTS A QUALIFIED INTERPRETER, PHYLLIS OGATA, AND THAT QUALIFICATION REQUIREMENTS AND ALL REQUIRED PROCEDURES HAVE BEEN FOLLOWED. THE COURT DENIED THE APPLICATION IN WHOLE. ORDER SIGNED, AND ENDORSED-FILED COPY MAILED TO APPLICANT. JUDGE: JOHN K. STEWART; CLERK: R. VELUZ; MATTER NOT REPORTED. (206)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jul-11-2016 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings
Off Calendar Jun-28-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - MEIKO SAITO, TEL. NO. 1-415-531-0662, PLAINTIFF APPEARING IN PROPER;
ERI MINOURA, TEL. NO. 1-650-289-9968, JAPANESE INTERPRETER FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. MARINA B. PITTS OF THE LAW OFFICES OF LEONE & ALBERTS, TEL. NO. 1-925-974-8600 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Defendant San Francisco Community College District's Motion for judgment on the pleadings on both claims alleged in the second amended complaint is granted without leave to amend as to both claims. Ms. Saito's general negligence claim fails as a matter of law because the claim is not based on a statute as required by Government Code section 815. Moreover, the negligence claim appears to be time barred by Government Code section 945.6(a)(2) and Ms. Saito has not provided a reason to delay accrual of the statute of limitations. Because it does not appear reasonably possible that Ms. Saito will be able to allege either a cognizable or timely claim for negligence, she is not given leave to try to do so. On April 14, 2016, the District's motion for judgment on the pleadings as to the intentional tort claim was granted without leave to amend. Ms. Saito has not provided any reason why that order should be disturbed. Order and Judgment signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR # 5976 =302/hk

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar May-02-2016 Continued To Jul-27-16 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion And Motion For An Award Of Sanctions Against Plaintiff Pursuant To Ccp 128.5
Off Calendar Apr-14-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Plaintiff Mieko Saito telephone number 415-531-0662 in Propria Persona.
Marina Pitts of Law Offices of Leone & Alberts telephone number 925-974-8600 for Defendant San Francisco Community College District.
Minoru Akuhara (Japanese Interpreter) also present.

The Interpreter's oath was administered. The Court explained the tentative ruling to Ms. Saitko. The Court confirmed its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT SAN FRANCISCO COMMUNITY COLLEGE DISTRICT'S Motion For An Award Of Sanctions Against Plaintiff Pursuant To CCP 128.5 is denied. There has been no showing of compliance with CCP 128.7 procedures. The motion seeks to invoke CCP 128.5 without acknowledging that section 128.5 requires compliance with section 128.7 and despite the fact that the January 22, 2016 letter by the District's counsel stated that the District would file a 128.7 motion. Moreover, the District has submitted insufficient evidence to support the amount of fees it seeks. Order signed in open Court.

Judge: Harold Kahn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976. (302/HK)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings
Off Calendar Apr-14-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Plaintiff Mieko Saito telephone number 415-531-0662 in Propria Persona.
Marina Pitts of Law Offices of Leone & Alberts telephone number 925-974-8600 for Defendant San Francisco Community College District.
Minoru Akuhara (Japanese Interpreter) also present.

The Interpreter's oath was administered. The Court explained the tentative ruling to Ms. Saitko. The Court confirmed its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT SAN FRANCISCO COMMUNITY COLLEGE DISTRICT's Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings on all claims in the first amended complaint is granted without leave to amend as to the claims for intentional tort and fraud and granted with twenty days leave to amend as to the general negligence claim. In her opposition memorandum plaintiff Meko Saito acknowledges that her intentional tort and fraud claims should be "deleted." Ms. Saito's general negligence claim is insufficiently pled because: 1) it does not allege that it is based on a statute as required by Government Code 815 and, 2) absent some reason to delay accrual of the statute of limitations, it appears to be barred by Government Code section 945.6(a)(2). If she can do so in good faith, Ms. Saito is granted leave to file a second amended complaint alleging a negligence claim that is based on a statute and that is not time-barred. Order signed in open Court.

Judge: Harold Kahn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976. (302/HK)
